confidence.disclosure: Route reconstructed from al-Tabari's annalistic history, al-Baladhuri's Futuh al-Buldan, and al-Waqidi's Futuh al-Sham. The early Ridda Wars stops and the famous desert march from Iraq to Syria (634) are well attested in multiple chronicles, but month-level dating is uncertain for most stops and the exact route of the desert crossing is debated. Battle locations and major cities are secure; intermediate desert stations are omitted where not firmly attested.

sources: Fred Donner, The Early Islamic Conquests (1981)

journey.event: Born in Mecca into the Banu Makhzum clan of Quraysh around 592 CE.
Commanded the Meccan cavalry whose flanking charge turned the Battle of Uhud against the Muslims.
Converted to Islam and joined the Prophet Muhammad in Medina.
Assumed command after the deaths of the three appointed leaders and extricated the Muslim force from a Byzantine-Ghassanid army.
Led one of the columns into Mecca during its conquest by the Prophet Muhammad.
Appointed by Abu Bakr to lead the main army against the apostate tribes at the outbreak of the Ridda Wars.
Defeated the prophetic claimant Tulayha of the Banu Asad at the Battle of Buzakha.
Destroyed the army of the rival prophet Musaylimah at the Battle of Yamama, ending the Ridda Wars in central Arabia.
Crossed into Sasanian Iraq and defeated a Persian force at the Battle of Chains (Dhat al-Salasil).
Defeated a combined Arab-Persian force at the Battle of Ullais on the lower Euphrates.
Captured the Lakhmid capital of Hira after the Persian garrison withdrew.
Captured the fortified city of Anbar on the Euphrates.
Took the oasis of Ayn al-Tamr west of the Euphrates.
Marched south to relieve Iyad ibn Ghanm and captured the oasis of Dumat al-Jandal.
Set out on the famous six-day waterless desert march from Iraq to the Syrian frontier.
Emerged from the desert crossing at the oasis of Suwa in the Palmyrene region.
Joined the Muslim armies operating in the Hauran and took the city of Bosra.
Won the decisive Battle of Ajnadayn against the Byzantine field army in Palestine.
Defeated Byzantine forces at the Battle of Fahl near the Jordan valley.
Took part in the six-month siege and capture of Damascus in September 635.
Operated around Homs as the Byzantines regrouped under Heraclius.
Commanded the Muslim army to a decisive victory over the Byzantines at the Battle of Yarmuk, breaking Byzantine power in Syria.
Campaigned in northern Syria and took Qinnasrin after Yarmuk.
Participated in the capture of Aleppo in northern Syria.
Dismissed from command by Caliph Umar around this time; retired to Homs.
Died in Homs around 642 CE; his tomb remains there to this day.
